archetype: Pastel & Soft mixed with Swiss & High-Contrast

visual_elements.style: Clean editorial layout mixed with playful handmade elements. Swiss editorial design, Notion simplicity, Comic-book storytelling, Hand-drawn annotations.
visual_elements.doodles: SVG hand-drawn arrows, sticky-note style illustrations, paper-cut doodles, sketch annotations.
visual_elements.borders: Solid 2px dark borders (#1A1A24) for cards, buttons, and specific sections to evoke comic-book styling.
visual_elements.shadows: Hard offset shadows (e.g., 4px 4px 0px #1A1A24) for a tactile, notebook/paper feel.

layout_spacing.grid: Bento grids for cards, asymmetrical layouts for text, generous whitespace.

sections.name: HERO
sections.layout: Full-screen (min-h-screen). Large heading, subhead, B&W portrait with an outlined frame (e.g., comic book style thick border), CTA button. Floating SVG doodles positioned absolute, animating with Framer Motion.

content.headline: Welcome to the World of Disruption
content.subhead: Human Thinking. AI Speed. Real Results.

sections.name: ABOUT
sections.layout: Two columns on desktop, single on mobile. 'Hello, I'm Shashank Saurav' (handwritten font for name). Origin story narrative with scroll-triggered reveals, hand-drawn arrows pointing to text.

content.title: Hello, I'm Shashank Saurav
content.narrative: Origin story narrative with scroll-triggered reveals and hand-drawn arrows.

sections.name: JOURNEY
sections.layout: Interactive winding timeline with checkpoints. Winding path SVG or vertical line in the background. Content boxes alternate sides on desktop. Superhero imagery floating.

sections.name: COLLECTIONS
sections.layout: Premium interactive project cards. Bento grid or horizontal scroll. Cards have hard offset shadows, hover lift (-translate-y-2), image zoom on hover.

sections.name: FAQ
sections.layout: Playful accordion. Thick borders, yellow accents on active items.

questions: What do you mean by disruption?

sections.name: THANK_YOU
sections.layout: Centered layout. Large B&W portrait, playful arrows pointing down to the contact section.

content.text: Thanks for making it this far.

sections.name: CONTACT
sections.layout: Grid layout for contact cards (Email, Phone, LinkedIn, Calendly) with click-to-copy functionality. Beside it, a clean contact form.

content.title: Let's do some great work together.

animations.scroll: GSAP-style scroll animations using Framer Motion (whileInView). Staggered reveals for lists and cards.
animations.hover: Microinteractions on buttons and cards (scale up slightly, shadow increases). Image scale up within overflowing hidden container.
animations.doodles: Parallax effect on floating doodles (use useScroll and useTransform from framer-motion).

components.buttons: Thick 2px solid dark borders (#1A1A24), bold text, primary (#6B88B3) or accent (#F6D34B) backgrounds, hard offset shadows (e.g., box-shadow: 4px 4px 0px #1A1A24).
components.cards: White or light backgrounds, 2px borders, hard offset shadows, generous padding.
components.accordion: Use Shadcn accordion, but customize with thick borders and playful font choices.

instructions_to_main_agent: IMPORTANT: Write React components using .jsx or .js extension, NOT .tsx.
Update tailwind.config.js to include the custom colors (primary: #6B88B3, secondary: #DDE6F5, accent: #F6D34B) and fonts (cabinet, space-mono, caveat, inter).
Add the font imports to src/index.css.
Use framer-motion for all scroll and hover animations. Add floating SVGs (arrows, stars, underlines) positioned absolute in sections to create the 'notebook' feel.
For the timeline, create an SVG path that weaves down the page, or use a vertical line with horizontal branches for an easier responsive approach. Mobile should be a straight vertical timeline.
All interactive elements MUST have data-testid attributes.
Install lucide-react for standard icons, but use custom SVG paths for the 'hand-drawn' feel where possible.
Use the handwritten font (Caveat) for annotations, arrows labels, and key highlight phrases (like 'How I moved in life', 'Shashank Saurav', 'of my latest work').
The UI should feel like a 'Neo-brutalist' mixed with a 'Strategist Notebook'—use hard shadows, thick borders, and playful typography pairing.
